title: Internal fault when cross-class calling with DPI
body:
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

Can you please attach an example that shows the issue or missing feature?

```verilog
package pyhdl_if;

    typedef chandle PyObject;
    import "DPI-C" context function PyObject _pyhdl_if_PyTuple_GetItem(input PyObject p0, input longint unsigned p1);

    function PyObject PyTuple_GetItem(input PyObject p0, input longint unsigned p1);
        return _pyhdl_if_PyTuple_GetItem(p0, p1);
    endfunction

class py_object;
    function new(PyObject obj);
    endfunction
endclass

class py_tuple;

    function py_object get_item(int idx);
        py_object ret = new(PyTuple_GetItem(null, longint'(idx)));
        return ret;
    endfunction

endclass

endpackage
```

The testcase above shows the internal fault top-of-trunk. This code works on 5.040.

What output from that test indicates it is wrong, and what is the correct or expected output?  (Or, please make test self-checking if possible.)

```
%Error: Verilator internal fault, sorry. Suggest trying --debug --gdbbt
%Error: Command Failed ulimit -s unlimited 2>/dev/null; exec /tools/verilator/5.043-20251125/bin/verilator_bin --binary pyhdl_pp_8.sv
```

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

```
% verilator --binary pyhdl_pp_8.sv
```

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?  Did you try it with other simulators?
I can reproduce this with a build from 'main'. This code (and the rest) works on 5.040, as well as other simulators
```
Verilator 5.043 devel rev v5.042-106-g9155e2529
```


What OS and distribution are you using? Ubuntu 24.04

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself? Yes
